checks.a_analysis: The output contains only information present in the source document, with the exception of metadata and alt-text which are permitted pipeline additions. No fabricated headings or body text were found.
The URL 'http://it.nc.gov/gicc' provided in the output correctly matches the source document. No other contact information is present in either document.

The output includes all primary sections and headers from the source document, including the header, main table items, committee list, meeting dates, and website information. No entire pages or primary sections were omitted.
The main table contains all 11 numbered agenda items as rows, matching the source. While the content of item 8 is reformatted into a list, the primary row count of the agenda structure is preserved.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: In the section for '8. Committee Reports', the output fails to include the names of the speakers (GICC MEMBER or GUEST column) associated with each sub-committee (Ryan Draughn, Kathryn Clifton, John Farley, Doug Newcomb, Dan Madding, Stan Duncan). Additionally, two specific voting items ('Vote: Standard for Parcel Data Content (Summary)' and 'Vote: LGC Bylaws Revised') are entirely omitted. This represents a substantial loss of informational content for this section.
checks.b_evidence_source: Item 8 includes sub-points and speakers: 'Statewide Mapping Advisory Committee Ryan Draughn', 'o Vote: Standard for Parcel Data Content (Summary)', 'Local Government Kathryn Clifton', 'o Vote: LGC Bylaws Revised', 'State Government GIS Users John Farley', 'Federal Interagency Doug Newcomb', 'Technical Advisory Dan Madding', 'Management & Operations Stan Duncan'
checks.b_evidence_output: The list element at reading_order 7 contains only the names of the committees: 'Statewide Mapping Advisory Committee', 'Local Government', 'State Government GIS Users', 'Federal Interagency', 'Technical Advisory', 'Management & Operations'. The names of the individuals and the voting sub-bullets are missing.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Apart from the major omissions noted in C5, the text content largely matches the source. Minor formatting choices (like the loss of italics on 'AddressNC' or hyperlinks on 'Minutes') are acceptable within the scope of normalization and document conversion.

checks.a_analysis: The content appears unique throughout the JSON output. No paragraphs, list items, or table rows are repeated unnecessarily compared to the source document.

The heading hierarchy follows a logical progression from H1 for the agency/title to H2 for 'AGENDA'. While there are two H1 tags, this is acceptable normalization for accessibility. There are no skipped levels (e.g., H1 to H3).

The table structure is corrupted in row 8 ('Committee Reports'). In the source PDF, this row contains a nested list of committees in the second column and corresponding presenter names in the third column (e.g., Ryan Draughn, Kathryn Clifton). The JSON output represents row 8 as a single row with an empty third cell, and the presenters' names are entirely missing from the structure. This destroys the structural alignment between committees and their respective members provided in the original document.
checks.b_evidence_source: Agenda item 8 shows 'Committee Reports' with a list of committees in the second column and corresponding names like 'Ryan Draughn' and 'Kathryn Clifton' in the third column.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order is materially broken. The sub-items for 'Committee Reports' (extracted as a separate list in element 7) are placed after the completion of the entire table (element 6). This means logically, these sub-items appear after agenda items 9, 10, and 11, disrupting the intended flow of the agenda. Additionally, the header logo is moved from the top of the document to the very last position (element 11).
checks.b_evidence_source: Committee sub-items appear under agenda item 8, before items 9-11.
checks.b_evidence_output: The list of committees (reading_order 7) appears after the table which contains items 9, 10, and 11 (reading_order 6).

checks.a_analysis: The nested list under 'Committee Reports' is converted incorrectly. The source contains sub-bulleted items (indicated by circles) for 'Vote' actions (e.g., 'Vote: Standard for Parcel Data Content'). These nested items are entirely omitted from the JSON list structure, resulting in a loss of the list's hierarchical meaning.
checks.b_evidence_source:
o Vote: Standard for Parcel Data Content (Summary)
o Vote: LGC Bylaws Revised
checks.b_evidence_output: The 'nested_list' fields for all items in the committee list are null, and the 'Vote' text is missing.

checks.a_analysis: The conversion fails to capture or preserve hyperlinks present in the source document. Text such as 'Minutes' and the 'Vote' items in the source are active blue links. Furthermore, the website URL at the bottom of the page ('http://it.nc.gov/gicc') is rendered as plain text within a paragraph rather than as a functional link object.
checks.b_evidence_output: "text": "3. Approval of Minutes of May 11th Meeting"; "text": "(New) GICC Website: http://it.nc.gov/gicc"

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the document is significantly altered in the conversion. In the source PDF, the committee reports (Item 8) are presented as a structured part of the main agenda table, with committee names in the middle column visually aligned with their respective presenters in the right column. In the HTML output, this organization is dismantled: the table row for Item 8 is left incomplete, and the list of committees is moved to a separate bulleted list below the table. This rearrangement breaks the visual flow and the intended association between the committees and the individuals reporting on them.
checks.b_evidence_source: Item 8 'Committee Reports' includes a nested list (e.g., 'Statewide Mapping Advisory Committee', 'Local Government') visually aligned with names in the third column ('Ryan Draughn', 'Kathryn Clifton').
checks.b_evidence_output: The table row for Item 8 has an empty 'GICC MEMBER or GUEST' column, and the committee names are moved to a standalone bulleted list following the table, losing the visual association with the reporters.
checks.b_evidence_location: Item 8, middle of page 1 / main content area

checks.a_analysis: The table structure for Item 8 is visually broken. In the PDF, Item 8 contains multiple rows of sub-items (committees) aligned with corresponding members/guests. In the HTML, the table rendering fails for this section: the 'GICC MEMBER or GUEST' column for row 8 is empty, and the sub-items are extracted from the table and rendered as plain text bullet points below it. This breaks the row-column relationship established in the source, making the table data for this section unreadable as a table.
checks.b_evidence_source: Table showing Item 8 with multiple sub-items in the middle column and corresponding names in the right column.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML table shows row 8 with a blank third column, and the committee names appear as a separate list below the table structure. The reporter names (Draughn, Clifton, etc.) are entirely missing from this section of the rendered HTML.

checks.a_analysis: The primary meaningful image, the NCGICC logo, is present in the HTML output. While its position has moved from the top header in the PDF to the bottom of the content in the HTML, the image is rendered correctly and remains meaningful. No other meaningful images (charts, photos) are present in the source to be missing.

No body content is visually duplicated. While titles and section headings ('North Carolina', 'AGENDA') appear in both the navigation 'Contents' sidebar and the main body, this is standard accessible design and not a content duplication defect. The agenda table and meeting details appear only once in the body.
